mnemonics: SPECTRUM: Stationary Processes Exhibit Coherent Transformations Relating Underlying Mechanisms
The PSD is your Frequency Bin (like a histogram for frequencies, showing power in each bin)
keyPoints: The spectral density function (or power spectrum) decomposes the variance of a stationary time series into contributions from different frequencies.
For a stationary process, the autocovariance function and the spectral density function form a Fourier transform pair.
A flat spectrum (white noise) indicates no serial correlation, while peaks indicate strong periodic components at those frequencies.
commonMistakes: Confusing the spectrum with the raw data plot; the spectrum is in the frequency domain, not the time domain.
Assuming a process is non-stationary because its spectrum has a peak; peaks indicate periodicity, not non-stationarity.
Forgetting that the spectrum is defined only for stationary processes, as variance must be constant over time.
